title: Advertising requirements for drugs and medical devices - Canada.ca
content:
## Advertising requirements for drugs and medical devices
- Health  product advertising
- Preclearance  process overview
- Health Canada’s  role

## Health product advertising
Health  product advertising directly or indirectly promotes the sale or use of any  health product. This can be done by any means including:
Only health  products that have been authorized for sale by Health Canada may legally be  advertised in Canada. These products include:
- natural  health products
- biologics  and biosimilars
- veterinary  health products
- opioids  and other controlled substances
Additional regulatory restrictions apply to  advertisements of:
- opioids and other controlled substances
It is the  responsibility of market authorization holders (manufacturers and distributors)  and advertisers to ensure that their advertising complies with the relevant  laws.

## Preclearance process  overview
Industry compliance with advertising preclearance is voluntary for all  health products except opioids. Preclearance is mandatory for opioids. We  strongly encourage all market authorization holders and advertisers to have the  materials for their authorized health products precleared prior to  dissemination.
- You may find details about the advertising preclearance system, contact information and roles by consulting the List of Canadian Advertising  Preclearance Agencies .

## Health Canada's role
Health  Canada is the national regulatory authority for health product advertisements.  We:
- administer       the: Food and Drugs Act Controlled Drugs        and Substances Act related Regulations
- Controlled Drugs        and Substances Act
- oversee       regulated advertising activities
- provide       policies to regulate marketed health products
- develop       guidelines for the interpretation of the Regulations
- set       standards for health product advertising materials to help advertisers       produce messages that are not: false deceptive misleading
Our  web-based platform Stop  illegal marketing of drugs and devices :
- raises awareness about illegal marketing of drugs  and medical devices
- educates health care providers on the rules  governing health product advertising in Canada
- provides a quick and easy tool to report  suspected misleading marketing practices
Visit  the compliance  and enforcement page for more details. Contact the Section for  Transparency and Advertising Regulatory Surveillance in Health Canada's Marketed Health Products Directorate  at drug-device-marketing@canada.ca .
- Preclearance  agencies
- Policies and guidance documents
- Controlled Drugs and  Substances Act
